response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with over 15 years of experience, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design at a principal level — none of which appear in his history. His closest relevant work (low-latency DLang microservices, C/C++ at National Instruments, an audio DSP thesis) demonstrates systems-level competence but is far from silicon performance architecture, and there is no deep-learning training or inference optimization experience.
strengths: Genuine low-latency systems experience: asynchronous I/O, fibers, and caching on services handling millions of requests per second
C/C++ and hardware-adjacent exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI instrumentation tooling, robotics, wave field synthesis thesis)
Long, self-driven engineering track record with open-source leadership and strong testing culture
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience whatsoever
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator performance background required by the role
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not a principal-level hardware performance track

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware-software co-design
No AI/deep-learning or GPU workload optimization experience
Low-latency systems and C/C++ performance work
